acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_OVERPROVES_POST_HARRY_TURN] In the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:335 and ending near snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:385, a cold reader can identify three distinct jobs only once each: Hermione remembers the folio discovery, she recognizes what her withholding looked like from Draco's side, and she lands a forward constraint about returning. If the passage states in new abstract wording more than once after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:357 that she protected herself by turning fear into work, the test fails.

[CH17_POST_TURN_SPRAWL] Pass if the post-Harry section (snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4737 onward) articulates no more than one new insight beyond what Harry's dialogue already established, and reaches the chapter's closing beat within 25-30 lines of Harry's departure rather than 60. Fail if two or more passages still paraphrase the same 'retreated into analysis' insight in different language.
[SPECIFIC_PARTICULAR_CH17] Pass if chapter 17 contains zero instances. Fail if any remains.
[THE_WAY_CH17] Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one 'the way' construction. Fail if more than one remains.
[R-13] Ch17 no longer contains an explicit narratorial statement that Harry has always been good at giving people space.
[R-17] Ch17 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.
[R-21] Ch17 has no more than 5 resonance references, each tied to a new observation.
[R-40] Ch17 has em-dashes in no more than 15 prose lines (<=8%).
[R-58] Ch17 has no more than 32 She-opened sentences (<=7%).
[R-73] Ch17 no longer uses 'channel(s) that no longer connected to anything.'

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:335 and ending near snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:385, a cold reader can identify three distinct jobs only once each: Hermione remembers the folio discovery, she recognizes what her withholding looked like from Draco's side, and she lands a forward constraint about returning. If the passage states in new abstract wording more than once after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:357 that she protected herself by turning fear into work, the test fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's decisive insight lands in the Harry scene, then the post-visit movement spends too much page weight translating the same conclusion into fresh abstract language. By the time Hermione reaches the sofa aftermath, the text has already established that her withholding was a self-protective attempt to control uncertainty; the later pages keep re-proving that diagnosis instead of letting the folio memory, scar recall, and return-constraint create a cleaner next turn. This reads as structural recurrence and overstatement after arrival rather than necessary aftermath.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-shape the aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:335, with special attention to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:345, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:383. Keep one concrete memory of the folio discovery, one Draco-side consequence beat, and the forward constraint at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:373, but cut or fold the repeated abstract restatements so the scene moves from Harry's diagnosis to one sharpened self-recognition and a new decision-pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the post-Harry section (snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4737 onward) articulates no more than one new insight beyond what Harry's dialogue already established, and reaches the chapter's closing beat within 25-30 lines of Harry's departure rather than 60. Fail if two or more passages still paraphrase the same 'retreated into analysis' insight in different language.

acceptance_test.excerpt: 4737: She thought about what Harry had said. Not the whole of it — the whole of it was too much, the kind of comprehensive truth that she'd need to take apart and examine piece by piece before she could hold it all at once. But the centre of it. The thing he'd said that had landed like a stone in water and was still sinking.

evidence.excerpt: 4737: She thought about what Harry had said. Not the whole of it — the whole of it was too much, the kind of comprehensive truth that she'd need to take apart and examine piece by piece before she could hold it all at once. But the centre of it. The thing he'd said that had landed like a stone in water and was still sinking.

4747: But Harry was right. The verification hadn't been about rigour. It had been about time — time she'd needed not to understand the mechanism but to decide what she felt about it. Time to sit inside the doubt privately, to turn the question over without anyone watching, to figure out whether what she felt for Draco Malfoy existed independent of a sentient house's intervention before she had to say it out loud and let the saying make it real.

4759: What she'd actually done was simpler and less forgivable. She'd been afraid. She'd found something that threatened to dissolve the realest thing she'd felt in years, and instead of sitting in that fear with the person who shared it, she'd retreated to the only place she'd ever felt safe: the work. The analysis. The frame where she controlled the variables. She'd made herself the researcher and him the data, not because she didn't care about him but because caring about him without certainty was the one thing her considerable capabilities couldn't help her do.

4785: She was angry because she'd had something. Something that had reached into the parts of her she'd sealed off after the war and the divorce and the years of competence as the load-bearing wall of her identity. Something that had been real in her body — not just in her mind, not just in the analysis, but in the specific physical fact of his wrist under her fingers and his breath catching when her diagnostic spells went deep and the resonance humming between them like a frequency only they could hear. And instead of trusting it — instead of bringing him the doubt and sitting in it together, two people inside the same uncertainty — she'd done the thing she always did. She'd retreated behind the glass. She'd researched. She'd verified. She'd built the careful conversation she'd planned to have, the one with the answers pre-loaded, the one designed to let her present the problem without being seen inside it.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
4737: She thought about what Harry had said. Not the whole of it — the whole of it was too much, the kind of comprehensive truth that she'd need to take apart and examine piece by piece before she could hold it all at once. But the centre of it. The thing he'd said that had landed like a stone in water and was still sinking.
4738: 
4739: _You weren't protecting him. You were protecting yourself._
4740: 
4741: She thought about the bonding texts. The night she'd found them — alone in the deep archive, Draco asleep two floors above, the Mark's resonance telling her he was dreaming, the specific quality of the ache that meant his sleep was restless. She'd read the texts three times. The first time with the rapid comprehension she brought to any new information. The second time slowly, with her analytical framework engaged, cross-referencing what the texts described against what she'd been experiencing. The third time with her hands shaking, because the mechanism the texts described — amplification of genuine emotional resonance, the house reading the bond between its inhabitants and strengthening it, gating access to deeper knowledge based on the connection's depth — matched everythin...

findings.problem: After the Harry conversation ends at line 4728, the chapter spends approximately 60 lines (4737-4791) restating in interior monologue what Harry already articulated in dialogue. Harry's diagnosis — 'You weren't protecting him. You were protecting yourself. From having to sit in the uncertainty before you had an answer.' — lands definitively at lines 4667-4668. The subsequent interior section recapitulates this insight repeatedly: line 4747 ('the verification hadn't been about rigour'), line 4759 ('she'd made herself the researcher and him the data'), line 4785 ('she'd retreated behind the glass. She'd researched. She'd verified.'). Each restating is well-written but does the same dramatic work as the prior one. The chapter's earned emotional force dissipates into explanatory surplus.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4737-4791 by at least 40%. Keep ONE interior passage that extends beyond Harry's diagnosis — the passage about the kiss (lines 4753-4755, 'He'd kissed her without knowing. She'd kissed him knowing.') is the strongest because it adds a concrete new cost Harry didn't name. Cut or radically compress the passages that merely restate 'she retreated into analysis instead of vulnerability' in successively cleaner language. End the chapter closer to the resonance-pulse beat at line 4789 without the extended thesis restatement.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains zero instances. Fail if any remains.

evidence.excerpt: 4713: They sat. The silence resettled. Harry picked up his container again and ate and didn't look at her, giving her the privacy of his inattention while she reassembled something. He was good at that. He'd always been good at that — the specific generosity of a person who didn't need to see you at your worst in order to know you'd been there.

4741: She thought about the bonding texts. The night she'd found them — alone in the deep archive, Draco asleep two floors above, the Mark's resonance telling her he was dreaming, the specific quality of the ache that meant his sleep was restless. She'd read the texts three times. The first time with the rapid comprehension she brought to any new information. The second time slowly, with her analytical framework engaged, cross-referencing what the texts described against what she'd been experiencing. The third time with her hands shaking, because the mechanism the texts described — amplification of genuine emotional resonance, the house reading the bond between its inhabitants and strengthening it, gating access to deeper knowledge based on the connection's depth — matched everything. The rooms that opened only when they were together. The Mark responding to her touch specifically. The growing awareness of his proximity and his pain. All of it consistent with architecture designed centuries ago to forge bonds between the house's chosen inhabitants.

4781: She thought about a man who held very still during examinations and called it trust. She thought about risotto and translations and the particular quality of his laugh when she caught him off guard, rare and startled and over quickly, as if he'd been surprised by his own capacity for it.

4785: She was angry because she'd had something. Something that had reached into the parts of her she'd sealed off after the war and the divorce and the years of competence as the load-bearing wall of her identity. Something that had been real in her body — not just in her mind, not just in the analysis, but in the specific physical fact of his wrist under her fingers and his breath catching when her diagnostic spells went deep and the resonance humming between them like a frequency only they could hear. And instead of trusting it — instead of bringing him the doubt and sitting in it together, two people inside the same uncertainty — she'd done the thing she always did. She'd retreated behind the glass. She'd researched. She'd verified. She'd built the careful conversation she'd planned to have, the one with the answers pre-loaded, the one designed to let her present the problem without being seen inside it.

4812: He knew what this was. Not withdrawal from a substance — the Malfoy vices ran colder — but from the specific addiction of someone paying sustained attention to you with care. He'd survived this kind of withdrawal once, after the war, when even Theo kept a measured distance and his mother's visits carried the weight of monitoring. He'd survived by contracting his world until needing anyone was no longer a variable. The Manor and him. Neither requiring anything further.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4713: They sat. The silence resettled. Harry picked up his container again and ate and didn't look at her, giving her the privacy of his inattention while she reassembled something. He was good at that. He'd always been good at that — the specific generosity of a person who didn't need to see you at your worst in order to know you'd been there.

findings.problem: Chapter 17 clusters five instances: 'the specific generosity of a person' (4713), 'the specific physical fact' (4741), 'the particular quality of his laugh' (4781), 'the specific physical fact of his wrist' (4785), 'the specific addiction of someone paying sustained attention' (4812). The density is especially damaging because this chapter contains the novel's strongest dialogue scene (the Harry conversation), and the prose tic is concentrated in the weaker interior-monologue sections that follow.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE all five at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4713, 4741, 4781, 4785, 4812. At line 4713: the generosity is already specified by the clause — cut the label. At 4781: render the laugh directly rather than labeling its 'particular quality.' At 4812: 'the specific addiction of someone paying sustained attention to you with care' — this is a strong insight but the formula dulls it; restructure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one 'the way' construction. Fail if more than one remains.

evidence.excerpt: 4419: The Manor's library alone had been larger than this. Her room in the guest wing had been twice the size of her bedroom here. The corridors between them had been — variable, alive, occasionally conspiratorial, the house shortening the path between the library and her bed on nights she worked late, as if it understood her exhaustion and took pity on it. Or as if it wanted her in the library and was removing obstacles. She'd never been certain which, and the uncertainty had been part of the texture of living there, the way you might live with a cat whose affection you couldn't fully distinguish from its appetite.

4685: "I know you, though," Harry said. He wasn't looking at her. He was looking at the takeaway bag on the floor, or pretending to look at it, giving her the periphery of his attention while the centre of it was on her. "I know what you do when you're scared. You don't run. You organize. You collect data. You build a wall of information between yourself and the thing you're afraid of, and you stand behind it, and you call it thoroughness."

4820: He walked back through the east wing corridor because it was the most direct route and because the house was no longer offering alternatives. Every door along the corridor was open or closed and stayed that way — the guest wing where she'd slept, the morning room his mother used for tea, the narrow passage to the conservatory that neither of them had ever bothered with. He passed the drawing room. Its door was open too, had been open since the house stopped closing anything, standing wide on its hinges like a mouth mid-sentence. He didn't look in. He hadn't looked in all week, but he knew the room was there the way you know a loose tooth — by the shape of the gap, by the involuntary clench every time he walked past it.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4419: The Manor's library alone had been larger than this. Her room in the guest wing had been twice the size of her bedroom here. The corridors between them had been — variable, alive, occasionally conspiratorial, the house shortening the path between the library and her bed on nights she worked late, as if it understood her exhaustion and took pity on it. Or as if it wanted her in the library and was removing obstacles. She'd never been certain which, and the uncertainty had been part of the texture of living there, the way you might live with a cat whose affection you couldn't fully distinguish from its appetite.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
